title: geum aleppicum subvar. rugosum
intro: Geum aleppicum subvar. rugosum, also known as the rough avens, is a perennial plant species within the geum genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and rough-hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Geum aleppicum subvar. rugosum are thought to include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, as well as the reduction of inflammation and pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of health issues.
